does: This paper introduces S&P Top-K, a retraining-free framework that improves model steering by operating directly on encoder features from Sparse Autoencoders rather than modifying decoder representations. The method identifies Top-K encoder features correlated with a sensitive attribute or unwanted behavior, optionally aggregates them into a control axis, and applies orthogonal projection in the model's native embedding space. Testing on vision-language models (CelebA, FairFace) and large language models (Llama-3 8B), the approach achieves up to 3.2 times improvement in fairness metrics and 3.6 times reduction in problematic behaviors like aggressiveness and sycophancy compared to conventional SAE-based steering.
connects: This work extends the established line of research in mechanistic interpretability that uses Sparse Autoencoders to decompose model activations into interpretable features. While prior work on SAE-based steering typically modifies activations through decoder features and reweighting, this paper responds to the limitations of that decoder-centric approach by exploring the untapped potential of encoder features directly. It builds on foundational work in fairness and behavioral control in neural networks, but applies these objectives through a lightweight projection-based method rather than expensive fine-tuning or complex intervention schemes used in earlier model steering literature.
enables: By demonstrating that encoder-centric steering outperforms decoder-centric approaches, this work opens new directions for efficient and interpretable model control without additional training. The retraining-free nature of the method enables rapid iteration for deploying fairness and safety measures. Future work could extend orthogonal projection-based steering to other model architectures, investigate multi-attribute control through joint projections, or combine this approach with causal analysis of encoder features. The framework may also enable better understanding of how different layers' representations relate to specific model behaviors, supporting broader development of interpretable and controllable AI systems.
